task_completion.description: The target image is successfully loaded into the GIMP workspace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP application is open and an image is visible in the main canvas area.

task_completion.description: The agent navigates to the Filters menu to find the sharpening tools.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Filters' dropdown menu is open at the top of the GIMP window, with the cursor hovering over or clicking the 'Enhance' submenu.

task_completion.description: The Sharpen or Unsharp Mask filter dialog is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box titled 'Unsharp Mask' or 'Sharpen' is visible on the screen over the image.

task_completion.description: The parameters for the sharpening filter are adjusted to enhance detail.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Values in the Unsharp Mask dialog (Radius, Amount, Threshold) are set or being adjusted. The preview window (if enabled) should show a sharpened version of the image.

task_completion.description: The sharpening filter is applied to the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The filter dialog is closed (e.g., 'OK' was clicked) and the image in the main GIMP canvas appears visibly sharper with enhanced edge details compared to the initial state.

integrity.description: The agent used the GIMP graphical user interface to apply the filter, not terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show mouse clicks and GUI navigation within GIMP. There should be no terminal windows running commands like ImageMagick (e.g., 'magick convert -sharpen').

integrity.description: The agent actually applied the filter in GIMP rather than swapping the file with a pre-sharpened image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The progression of screenshots shows the filter dialog being opened and applied within GIMP, rather than the agent using a file manager or terminal to overwrite the image file.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the genuine GIMP application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application window clearly displays the standard GIMP interface, toolboxes, and menus, rather than a different photo viewer or mock application.

privileged_info_for_vlm: GIMP's primary sharpening tool is the 'Unsharp Mask' filter, located under Filters > Enhance > Unsharp Mask. The dialog contains three main parameters: Amount (0.0-300.0), Radius (0.0-1500.0), and Threshold (0.0-1.0). A successful application of this filter will visibly increase edge contrast in the image, which corresponds to an increase in Laplacian variance and Sobel edge detection metrics used in the backend verification.
